title: Verify restructuring works
description: Run import checks, syntax checks, make config to verify pipeline/analysis split

title: Docs + cleanup review + separation-of-concerns audit
description:
After data_loader + paper-script fixes land, do a holistic sweep:

1. Documentation walkthrough — every markdown file must be accurate against the current code layout and data flow. Files to reread with critical eye:
   - README.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, STATUS.md, REPLICATION_STATUS.md
   - notes/README.md, notes/CODE_ORGANIZATION.md, notes/table_replication.md
   - analysis/imagequality_classification/README.md
   - analysis/image_survey_classification/CHANGES.md (is it still relevant?)
   - scripts/SPLIT_DATA_README.md (will be deleted with oou, confirm)

2. File cleanup — identify and delete/move unnecessary files:
   - Stale logs/outputs/CSVs in classifier dirs (aggregated_misclassified_*.csv)
   - Old notebooks that no longer run
   - Orphaned __init__.py in empty trees
   - Any scripts referencing deleted paths

3. Separation-of-concerns audit — walk the code and flag any remaining violations:
   - pipeline/ should not consume all_data.json (it produces it)
   - analysis/ should not write to raw/ or intermediate/ (only analysis_output/ and models/)
   - No script should read from or write to both raw/ and intermediate/ and models/ in one pass
   - No second materialization layers between all_data.json and consumers (rule #3)
   - No hard-coded paths outside the Makefile variables
   - Every script that reads data should go through pipeline/data_loader.OrganoidDataset

Produce a punch list of violations found and fix each.
close_reason: Completed across separation-of-concerns work this branch: commits a613a4e (initial cleanup), a45ea5d (deletes/renames + B1/B3/B4), b668358/3688cfa/1e3495c/7f79d72 (C1/C2/C3+C4/C5 splits + dedup), f51794e (final cleanup of orphaned dirs).

title: Investigate R2 mask-data: identify uchicago-masks, masks-renamed, idor-masks origins
description: R2 mask-data has 21,905 files across 4 subdirs vs 3,184 local. Determine what each R2 mask set is, where it came from, and whether any are needed.

comments.author: nickross
comments.text:
R2 mask-data has 4 distinct datasets (21,905 total files):

1. idor-masks (2,148 files) = Local masks/manual source data
   - masks-batch-1: 1,035 (manual + threshold) — matches local exactly (365 manual + 670 threshold)
   - masks-batch-2_1: 372 (manual only) — matches local exactly
   - masks-batch-2_2: 741 (manual only) — matches local exactly
   - Missing from R2: local 'Manuais' dir (366 files) and 'Treshold' dir (670 files)

2. masks-renamed (5,154 files) = Predicted masks with normalized names
   - Organized BA1-4/Dy03-30, filenames like BA1_96_1_Dy03_A10_nosplit_nostitch_predmask.png
   - These are small PNG predicted masks (~1.5KB each)
   - Appears to be an older run of predicted masks (not matching current local predicted/)

3. uchicago-masks (10,391 files) = Overlays + predicted masks from UChicago pipeline
   - Organized batch1-4/day03-30/{image_mask_overlays,predicted_masks}
   - image_mask_overlays: overlay PNGs (~300KB each)
   - predicted_masks: predicted mask PNGs (~1.5KB each)
   - These are a DIFFERENT processing run from the current local pipeline

4. uchicago-masks-with-ratio (4,212 files) = Aspect-ratio masks
   - Flat directory, small PNG files (~800 bytes)
   - Filenames: BA1_96_1_Dy03_A2_nosplit_nostitch.png
   - Another processing variant

CONCLUSION: Only idor-masks corresponds to irreplaceable source data (hand-labeled). The other 3 are derived outputs from older pipeline runs. Local masks/manual has 2 extra dirs (Manuais + Treshold) not on R2.

title: Tighten ruff gate: enable phased-in rules + ruff format across repo
description: Follow-up to kbe. The initial ruff gate ignores a set of existing-offender rules (B905, B904, B028, B023, B007, E701, E702, E721, E741 — see pyproject.toml [tool.ruff.lint].ignore) and does NOT enforce 'ruff format' (repo predates it). Progressively: (1) clean each ignored rule category across active code and remove it from the ignore list; (2) run 'ruff format' repo-wide and add 'ruff format --check' back into 'make lint'; (3) expand lint scope to the currently-excluded dirs (pipeline/images/segmentation_mmseg under mmcv_env, cnn_lstm F821 per-file-ignore, legacy dirs). Do in small PRs to keep diffs reviewable. Owner: Nick.

title: Epic: rebuild paper_2026_04 against the data loader
description: Rename current analysis/paper_2026_04 → legacy_paper_2026_04 (frozen reference). Build a new paper_2026_04 from scratch that: (a) uses OrganoidDataset's public API (iter_organoids/organoid_label/include_growth=True) — no _organoids access, no reimplemented growth features. (b) uses centralized LABEL_TO_INT (fixes the inversion bug in legacy perday_image_study where 0=Acceptable contradicts every other script). (c) factors a shared metrics/plotting helper module. (d) reproduces the same outputs (Tables 1+3, Figs 5/8/10/11, perday results.json) so three_model_plot continues to work.

title: Map R2 bucket contents to local DATA_DIR directories
description: Create a detailed mapping showing which R2 files correspond to which local paths, what's missing on each side, and what's different.

comments.author: nickross
comments.text: Complete map written to DATA_MAP.md. Key findings: R2 uses 6 separate buckets vs local single tree; R2 missing 6 stitched images, 1036 masks, all derived outputs, and has stale all_data.json. R2 has ~20K extra files from older pipeline runs (uchicago-masks, masks-renamed).

title: New metabolites_train.py: use include_growth=True + dedup train_*
description: Two big wins over legacy: (1) Drop _get_growth_features and get_features_with_growth (130 lines). Just call ds.get_metabolite_features(split, day, include_growth=True, include_initial=True). (2) Factor train_lgbm_day + train_logreg_day (180 lines combined) into single train_with_grid_and_threshold(model_factory, param_grid, threshold_grid, scoring, scaler=None) → metrics. Both call sites become ~20 lines.
acceptance_criteria: results.json for Dy24/Dy28/Dy30 within ±0.01 balanced_accuracy of legacy values.

title: Investigate R2 image-data: reconcile 26,823 vs 26,829 local raw files
description: R2 has 6 fewer files than local. Find which files are missing and whether R2's batch/day directory structure matches the flat local layout.

comments.author: nickross
comments.text: R2 missing 6 files: Ba2 96_2 Dy30 D12(2)%(stitched) Z0-Z5.tif. Likely upload failure due to special chars. R2 has 0 extra files. All other 26,823 filenames match.

title: Rename: analysis/paper_2026_04/ → analysis/legacy_paper_2026_04/
description: git mv the entire directory. Update Makefile target 'analysis-paper-perday' to point to new location. Update notes/CODE_ORGANIZATION.md mention.
acceptance_criteria: Old scripts still importable from analysis.legacy_paper_2026_04.*; Makefile target unchanged in name but points to new path.

title: Add 'mode' field to cli.create_summary; remove dinov2 write_summary
description: train_model_dinov2.py has its own write_summary (lines 208-268, 60 lines) that's nearly identical to cli.build_results_table + cli.create_summary, except it adds 'used_fixed_splits: True' and 'mode: <preset>' to the JSON. Extend cli.create_summary to accept optional metadata kwargs and merge them into the summary dict; delete dinov2's write_summary.
acceptance_criteria: dinov2 calls build_results_table + create_summary directly; final_test_summary.json still contains 'mode' and 'used_fixed_splits' fields.

title: Audit analysis/paper_2026_04/*.py for schema-staleness
description:
STATUS.md claims paper replication (Table 1 metabolite means, Table 3 LightGBM Dy30 bal_acc=0.9444) hit exact match, but that must have been against an older all_data.json in the flat intermediate schema — the current normalized schema breaks every filter in data_loader, and several paper scripts also reach into records directly with rec.get('metabolites') etc.

Audit (after the data_loader fix): every script under analysis/paper_2026_04/ must run end-to-end against today's data/all_data.json and produce numbers matching the STATUS.md claims.

Files to check:
- descriptive_stats.py (Table 1)
- metabolite_boxplot.py (Fig 5)
- metabolites_train.py (Table 3) — has direct rec.get('metabolites') access at line ~119
- feature_importance.py (Fig 10)
- perday_image_study.py (Fig 7 — image model)
- three_model_plot.py (Fig 11)
- generate_splits.py

For any direct record-field access (bypassing OrganoidDataset accessors), switch to the new schema or route through the dataset API.
close_reason: Completed by 1e3495c (C3+C4): all paper_2026_04 scripts rewritten against pipeline.data_loader public API. metabolites_train uses include_growth=True; descriptive_stats uses iter_organoids; perday_image_study uses LABEL_TO_INT (fixes inversion bug). Old scripts frozen in legacy_paper_2026_04/.

title: Verify dinov2 trainer produces identical results before/after dedup
description: Run train_model_dinov2.py on a single day before and after refactoring, compare metrics_test.json byte-for-byte (or floating-point near-equal). If runs diverge, the run_phases / extract / summary changes are not behavior-preserving.
acceptance_criteria: Two runs of train_model_dinov2.py on the same input (single day, fixed seed, no GPU non-determinism) produce identical final_test_summary.json.

Move + minor cleanup: generate_splits, three_model_plot, feature_importance

Part of paper epic (2025-promega-mini-test-eyq). Owner: Nick. Source: PromegaPlan.docx Tables & Figures (Table #1-2, Nick).

description: Move train_epoch / eval_epoch / pretrain_shared_backbone / train_for_day into train.py. While moving: (1) collapse the dead 6-branch batch-unpacking conditionals to a single '*inputs, y = batch' line in train_epoch and eval_epoch. (2) Have eval_epoch return preds/labels arrays alongside its metrics dict; build per-organoid CSV rows in train_for_day from those instead of duplicating the eval loop in eval_epoch_detailed.

title: Create data.py: dataset + transforms + load_and_prepare_data

While moving: shift the metabolite zero-padding logic out of MetaboliteBranch.forward (model.py concern) into MultimodalRowDataset (data concern). Dataset already builds variable-length feature lists; pad to scaler dimension at __getitem__ time.

title: Merge extract_samples_by_day variants into data.py
description: train_model_dinov2.py has extract_samples_by_day (lines 65-92) that uses OrganoidDataset.get_image_paths to pull (img_paths, labels, mask_paths) for one (split, day, mode). train_model_accuracy.py uses train.run_training_for_day which has its own per-day extraction over the views dict. Move the dinov2 version into data.py as a public helper that handles both flows: (a) views dict with hard-split fractions (accuracy), (b) OrganoidDataset with fixed splits (dinov2). Should accept either an OrganoidDataset OR a views dict.
acceptance_criteria: data.extract_samples_by_day handles both call sites; both trainers use it; dinov2-local copy removed.

title: Investigate R2 metabolite-data: 4 files vs 1 local xlsx
description: R2 has 4 metabolite xlsx files, local only uses metabolite_data_07_23_25.xlsx. Determine if the extras are older versions or additional data.

comments.author: nickross
comments.text:
R2 has 4 xlsx files, local has 1. Mapping:
- R2 '072325 Metabolite Assays_All Plates with 1103 rerun.xlsx' (3,080,943 bytes) ≈ local 'metabolite_data_07_23_25.xlsx' (3,077,845 bytes) — same date, ~3KB size diff, likely same data with minor edits
- R2 '052225 Metabolite Data_batches 11 and 21.xlsx' — older/subset data (May 2025)
- R2 'Updated Metabolite Data Batches 11_21_22.xlsx' — updated subset
- R2 '384WellAnalysis Metabolite Plate Map and Sample coding.xlsx' — plate map reference doc
R2 extras appear to be earlier versions and reference docs. Local only has the final consolidated file plus derived metabolite_map.json.

title: Morphology features from masks -> all_data.json (incl. multifractal Complexity)
description:
Compute the lab's morphology descriptors from segmentation masks ourselves (do not trust the lab's precomputed values), store in all_data.json, and verify against the lab CSV. Mirrors the merged mask_area pattern. Context saved in agent memory: morphology-from-masks-task.

Features (lab morph_cols): Area, Circularity, Aspect Ratio, Solidity, Complexity, Feret. Only Area is computed today (mask_area_px/um2). NOTE: images.aspect_ratio is image-resize metadata, NOT morphometric AR.

Lab source = ImageJ macro Feature_extraction.ijm (RehenLab/MMM): 'area shape feret', Analyze Particles >=90000 um2, per-batch scale. Lab values = *_win cols in data/normalized/CONC_data_organoides_residualized_final.csv.

Validated already (skimage.regionprops vs lab, n=80): AR corr 0.92/1.1%, Solidity 0.95/0.5%, Circularity 0.93/4.7% (ImageJ vs skimage perimeter offset), Area ~0.4%. The 5 standard descriptors are ready.

Phase 1 (low-risk): pipeline step computing Area/AR/Solidity/Feret/Circularity via regionprops -> all_data.json + make verify-morphometry (copy the mask_area files: mask_edge_fraction.py, merge/normalized_records.py, data_loader accessor, backfill script, organoid_area.py).

Phase 2 (Complexity = multifractal spectrum width Delta-alpha): paper Methods + Zhao et al. 2023 (Chaos Solitons Fractals 173, 113719) = sandbox edge-based (SBEB) on the mask contour; complexity = range between max/min exponents. NOT a standard ImageJ descriptor; FracLac is the ImageJ multifractal plugin but uses box-counting (different algo). Implemented in the lab's custom Python (not public). OPEN: get lab's Complexity code / Supp Table S1, OR implement (B) SBEB from Zhao 2023 and/or (C) box-counting Delta-alpha, calibrate to Complexity_win. Was mid-experiment on B+C when tabled.
acceptance_criteria: Area/AR/Solidity/Feret/Circularity computed from masks, stored in all_data.json, verified vs lab CSV via make verify-morphometry; Complexity (multifractal Delta-alpha) implemented and shown to track Complexity_win.

title: Epic: dedupe train_model_dinov2.py vs train_model_accuracy.py
description: Both image-quality trainers share data.py / models.py / eval.py / plots.py / cli.py, but train_model_dinov2.py reimplements three pieces that should live in the shared modules. After consolidation, dinov2 should be ~150 lines (CLI + main only).

title: Create cli.py: argparse + config dict construction
description: Lift main()'s argparse block (lines 832-896) and the modality-flag normalization into cli.py. Returns the config dict consumed by every other module. Use --use-images/--use-metabolites with --images-only/--metabolites-only resolution.
acceptance_criteria: cli.get_config() returns dict matching current train_multimodal main()'s config.

title: Rewrite train_multimodal.py as <100-line orchestrator
description: main() loads config via cli, loads data via data.load_and_prepare_data, calls train.pretrain_shared_backbone + train.train_for_day per day, accumulates results, writes summary CSV + master CSV merge, calls plots.*.
acceptance_criteria: train_multimodal.py is <120 lines and contains only main() + summary-CSV merge logic.

title: Add common.py: shared compute_metrics + plotting helpers
description: Three legacy paper scripts (perday_image_study, metabolites_train, multimodal) each have a near-duplicate compute_metrics function with subtly different keys. Centralize: (a) compute_classification_metrics(y_true, y_pred, y_prob=None) → dict with stable key set (accuracy, balanced_accuracy, f1, recall, precision, confusion_matrix:{tn,fp,fn,tp}, roc_auc?). (b) plot_balanced_accuracy_by_day(series_dict, output_path) for any subset of {image, lgbm, logreg, combined} models.
acceptance_criteria: common.compute_classification_metrics and common.plot_balanced_accuracy_by_day exported; new paper modules consume them.

title: Paper Reproducibility: Generate all figures, tables, and numbers from all_data.json
description: Reproduce all analysis in the Promega ML Paper from all_data.json as the single data source. Each child ticket covers a specific figure, table, or computed number from the paper. All analysis must be self-contained and source from all_data.json. Code from the student branch (2026-winter-promega-final-submission) can be adapted but not copied wholesale. Temporary data from long-running processes may be saved outside the repo.

title: Data splits: reproduce train/val/test splits from all_data.json
description: Generate deterministic train/val/test splits (80/10/10) at the organoid level from all_data.json. The split must be shared across image, metabolite, and combined models. Must remove 36 bad entries (duplicates for split organoids), leaving 5,132 records. Source: all_data.json. Output: split JSONs. Student branch ref: split_data_reproducible.py. NOTE: The split files currently live in data/data_splits/. Verify these match the methodology described in the paper or regenerate.
close_reason: Done: data_loader.py + generate_splits.py + organoid_splits.csv (220 organoids, 158/18/44 train/val/test, seed=42)

title: Table: Legacy 3-backbone comparison (ViT, ResNet, EfficientNet)
description: Reproduce Table (tab:legacy_model_comparison): cross-model comparison of ViT, ResNet50, EfficientNet-B0. Metrics: Avg TNR (all), Early TNR (Dy3-10), Balanced Accuracy, Days with TNR=0, F1 (Not Acceptable). Paper values: EfficientNet Avg TNR=29.1%, Bal.Acc=59.0%. Source: all_data.json for images+labels, shared splits. This requires TRAINING 3 models (GPU, long-running). Student branch ref: train_model_accuracy.py, generate_efficientnet_summary.py. Output: CSV/table + trained model checkpoints (saved outside repo).
notes: Per-day EfficientNet run on GPU. Code matches student exactly (seed=1, val_acc selection, LR*0.1). Dy28=0.89 bal_acc. Early days show majority-class bias. No ViT/ResNet50 yet.

title: Figure: Per-day vs time-series balanced accuracy comparison
description: Reproduce Figure (perday_vs_timeseries.png): balanced accuracy across culture days at threshold=0.5 for per-day classifier vs time-series classifier. Paper values: per-day peaks at 0.76 (Day 15), 0.80 (Day 20.5), 0.87 (Day 30); time-series stays near/below 0.50 until late days. Source: all_data.json for images+labels. Requires training per-day EfficientNet models AND time-series (effnet_ts) models for each day. Student branch ref: run_per_day_study.py. GPU required, long-running.
notes: Per-day results done. Time-series model (CNN-LSTM or temporal attention) not yet implemented. Per-day figure generated.

title: Table: Metabolite LogReg vs LightGBM comparison
description: Reproduce Table (tab:metabolite_model_comparison): LR vs LightGBM aggregated across 11 days. Paper values: LightGBM Avg Bal.Acc=60.9%, Avg Recall(NA)=39.3%, Best Bal.Acc=94.4% (Day 30); LogReg Avg Bal.Acc=52.9%. Source: all_data.json metabolite records + labels + shared splits. Requires training 22 models (11 days x 2 algorithms). Student branch ref: train_metabolites_cpu.py, train_metabolites_logreg.py. CPU-only, moderate runtime.
close_reason: Done: LightGBM Dy30=0.9444 bal_acc matches paper. LR uses f1_weighted/saga/l1+l2 matching student code

title: Figure: LightGBM vs Logistic Regression balanced accuracy by day
description: Reproduce Figure (LightGBM_vs_Logistic_Regression.png): balanced accuracy by day for both metabolite models with shaded late-stage region. Source: results from metabolite model training. Can be generated from the same run as the metabolite comparison table.
close_reason: Done: analysis/metabolites/train.py generates LightGBM vs LR plot

title: Figure: LightGBM feature importance across days
description: Reproduce Figure (Feature Importance Graph.png): feature importance from LightGBM metabolite classifier at Days 6, 15, 24, 30. Circle=concentrations, triangle=differences. Key findings: glutamate/lactate important early, malate difference most important at Day 30. Source: trained LightGBM models from metabolite training. Student branch ref: extract_feature_importance.py.
close_reason: Done: analysis/metabolites/feature_importance.py generates figure from saved results

title: Figure: Three-model comparison (image, metabolite, combined)
description: Reproduce Figure (three_model.png): balanced accuracy comparison across days for image-only, metabolite-only, and combined models. Paper: image peaks at Day 15 (~0.76) and Day 30 (~0.88); metabolite strongest at Day 30 (~0.94); combined tracks metabolite early, ~0.76 late. Source: results from all three model families. This is the capstone figure requiring all model training to be complete.

title: Combined model: PCA image features + metabolite LightGBM
description: Train per-day combined LightGBM classifiers: 12 PCA-reduced EfficientNet embeddings + 10 metabolite features = 22-dim vector. GridSearchCV with StratifiedGroupKFold (3 folds), F1(NA) metric. Source: all_data.json for images+metabolites+labels. Requires pre-trained EfficientNet for embeddings. Student branch ref: train_combined_lgbm.py. GPU for embeddings, CPU for LightGBM.

title: Figure: Day 15 vs Day 17 overlay comparison (Day 17 drop)
description: Reproduce Figure (day15_vs_day17_overlays.png): comparison of a specific organoid at Day 15 vs Day 17, illustrating size change associated with the Day 17 drop. Source: all_data.json overlay image paths for a selected organoid. Output: side-by-side PNG.

These come from metabolite model evaluation metrics.

Document any GAPS where data is missing from all_data.json that the paper analysis requires. If gaps exist, write a spec for what needs to be added.

Source: all_data.json image paths + mask paths for a single organoid. Output: PNG strip figure. This requires selecting an example organoid and compositing its overlay images across days.

title: Figure: Stitched organoid + red scale bar example

title: Figure: Split organoid example

title: Merge step does not propagate edge_fraction into all_data.json
description:
**Where:** `pipeline/merge/merge_all_data.py` (step 16) — uses the wrong intermediate file as the image-map source.

**Symptom:** All 5,168 records in `data/all_data.json` have `images.edge_fraction = null`, even though step 11 (`pipeline/images/quality/mask_edge_fraction.py`) successfully computed edge fractions and wrote them to `intermediate/indexes/image_map_resized_512x384_predicted_overlay_edge.json`.

**Root cause:** The merge step pulls from `image_map_resized_512x384_predicted_overlay_ar_meanfill.json` (the post-step-15 mean-fill output), which doesn't carry the `edge_fraction` field. Step 11's output JSON (`..._overlay_edge.json`) is never consumed downstream.

**Evidence:**
- Pipeline log `logs/pipeline-20260429_1605.log:1495` confirms step 11 ran and updated the `_overlay_edge.json` file.
- File on disk: `/net/projects2/promega/2026_04_15_data/intermediate/indexes/image_map_resized_512x384_predicted_overlay_edge.json` (8.5 MB, populated).
- `python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('data/all_data.json')); from collections import Counter; print(Counter(type((v.get('images') or {}).get('edge_fraction')).__name__ for v in d.values()))"` → `Counter({'NoneType': 5168})`.

**Impact:** The IDOR partner-list verification (`analysis/verify_ba1_ba2_idor_list/verify.py`) cannot verify the claim that "images with edge_fraction > 0.05 were excluded from feature extraction" because the field is empty. Any analysis that wants to filter on edge_fraction (e.g., paper-replication scripts that should drop cut organoids on Dy24/28/30) is silently skipping the filter.

**Fix:** In step 16's merge logic, either (a) read from `_overlay_edge.json` instead of `_overlay_ar_meanfill.json`, or (b) merge the two intermediate files so edge_fraction joins the meanfill data before merge. Option (a) is simplest if the meanfill paths can be derived from the edge JSON.

**Acceptance:**
- After `make pipeline-all`, `data/all_data.json` has non-null `edge_fraction` for the records where step 11 computed one.
- `make analysis-verify-idor` reports a non-zero count of records with edge_fraction > 0.05, concentrated on Dy24/Dy28/Dy30 as the partner described.
close_reason: Fixed in pipeline/images/quality/mask_edge_fraction.py — now overwrites IMAGE_MAP_OVERLAY in place. After rerun: 5168/5168 records have edge_fraction populated, 127 records >0.05 concentrated on Dy24/28/30 as partner described.

title: Integrate amanda-time-model into main (resolve 25 merge conflicts)
description:
Merge origin/amanda-time-model (temporal CNN-LSTM + gradcam/attention explainability, LSTM heatmaps, confident-example finders, missed-organoid montages, new cohort splits + split tooling) into main.

DOES NOT MERGE CLEANLY — 25 conflicts (as of main 0ad6d65 / amanda abd4815; common base f4432bc; main +115 commits, amanda +20).

Content conflicts (both edited) — 5, all analysis/images/cnn_lstm/:
- analyze_misses.py, organoid_dataset.py, train_base_model.py, train_temporal_ablation_attn.py, train_temporal_ablation_lstm.py

Modify/delete conflicts — 20:
- analysis/images/cnn_lstm/train_organoid_lstm.py: DELETED in amanda, MODIFIED in main
- data/cohorts/expanded/* (10) + data/cohorts/idor/* (9) + scripts/splits/make_splits.py: DELETED in main, MODIFIED in amanda

Root cause: amanda branched from an older main that had data/cohorts/ + scripts/splits/ and an earlier cnn_lstm/. Since then main removed data/cohorts/ and scripts/splits/ entirely (neither exists on main now) and edited the same cnn_lstm files.

Decisions needed (not mechanical):
1. Keep main's removal of data/cohorts/ + scripts/splits/, or restore amanda's versions (her temporal work depends on them)?
2. Whose cnn_lstm edits win (reconcile both sides)?
3. train_organoid_lstm.py: keep (main) or accept amanda's deletion?

Recommended path: Amanda merges current main INTO amanda-time-model, resolves with her knowledge of both her code and what main retired, then opens a PR. Alternatively resolve on a branch here with explicit per-decision guidance. Do NOT auto-resolve blindly.
acceptance_criteria: amanda-time-model merged into main with all 25 conflicts resolved; cnn_lstm code and cohort/splits layout reconciled; result runs (make/scripts) and reviewers sign off.

title: Pipeline step 2: replace bare except with explicit NaN-row skip in metabolite_mapper
description:
**Where:** `pipeline/metabolites/metabolite_mapper.py:145-162`

**Problem:** The per-row loop is wrapped in `try/except Exception: logging.exception("Skipping row due to unknown error")`. On every pipeline run this fires for at least one row in `metabolite.xlsx` where `row["batch"]` is NaN, producing a scary `ValueError: cannot convert float NaN to integer` traceback in the log even though the row is correctly skipped.

**Observed:** `logs/pipeline-20260429_1605.log:20-28` — Step 2 runs to completion (4,154 entries, matches expected count), but the traceback at `metabolite_mapper.py:82` (`batch = str(int(row["batch"]))`) is logged verbatim because of the bare except.

**Fix:**
1. Pre-filter the dataframe before the loop: drop rows where `batch`, `starting plate`, `day`, or `96 well` are NaN/empty. Log the count of dropped rows once.
2. Remove the catch-all `except Exception` in the loop (or narrow to `(ValueError, KeyError)` and log as warning, not exception).

**Why this matters:** The blanket except hides any real bug introduced into `get_organoid_id` / `get_assay_dict` — every failure looks like "one more skipped NaN row". With ~25k metabolite rows processed, a regression could silently drop hundreds and the pipeline assertion at line 180 would still pass if the count happens to align.

**Acceptance:**
- Step 2 runs cleanly with no traceback in the log.
- Skipped-row count is logged as a single summary line.
- `metabolite_map.json` still has 4,154 entries on `raw/metabolite.xlsx`.

Feeds paper epic (2025-promega-mini-test-eyq). Owner: Nick. Source: PromegaPlan.docx outstanding to-dos ('Big one: Ablation tests for metabolite data').

title: Delete data_splits/ materialization (AGENTS.md rule #3 violation)
description:
AGENTS.md rule #3: all_data.json is the single source of truth. Do not materialize filtered/transformed data into separate JSON files for downstream models; use the data loader instead.

scripts/split_data_reproducible.py and scripts/split_data_no_stitch.py violate this. They write data_splits/{both,image}_{train,val,test}_{base,switch1,switch2,switch3}.json containing fully-materialized per-organoid views with absolute /net/projects2/promega paths baked in. Files break on any clone and drift from the true source.

Only consumer: analysis/imagequality_classification/train_model_accuracy_tony_dinov2.py (reads both_{train,val,test}_base.json).

Steps (after data_loader schema fix lands):
1. Delete data/data_splits/*.json (root data_splits/ already removed)
2. Add data_splits/ and data/data_splits/ to .gitignore
3. Rewrite train_model_accuracy_tony_dinov2.py to use OrganoidDataset + filters_for_mode('base') from pipeline/data_loader.py
4. Update analysis/imagequality_classification/run_tony_dinov2_fixed_splits.s to drop split-file args
5. Delete scripts/split_data_reproducible.py, scripts/split_data_no_stitch.py, scripts/SPLIT_DATA_README.md
6. Grep for any remaining references to data_splits/ and clean up

Note: filters_for_mode() covering base/switch1/switch2/switch3 is already added to pipeline/data_loader.py in this branch.
close_reason: data_splits/ already gone (.gitignored, dirs absent on disk). scripts/split_data_*.py and SPLIT_DATA_README.md deleted. Sole consumer train_metabolites.py frozen as metabolites_legacy/. data/data_splits/ also absent.

title: Fix data_loader.py: align with normalized all_data.json schema
description:
pipeline/data_loader.py was written against the flat in-memory intermediate dict inside merge_all_data.py (fields BA, dayID, processed.*, metabolites plural). The actual on-disk all_data.json is the nested normalized schema produced by normalized_records.OrganoidRecordBuilder (plate.batch, day.id, images.*, metabolite singular). Against today's all_data.json the data_loader returns zero organoids — every filter rejects every record.

Field translations needed:
- rec['BA'] -> rec['plate']['batch'] (note: batch is 'BA1 96_1' form, not just 'BA1')
- rec['dayID'] -> rec['day']['id']
- rec['processed']['img_path'] -> rec['images']['img_path']
- rec['processed']['mask_path'] -> rec['images']['mask_path']
- rec['processed']['overlay_path'] -> rec['images']['overlay_path']
- rec['metabolites'] -> rec['metabolite']
- survey/label shape needs verification against OrganoidRecordBuilder._build_surveys output
- _extract_organoid_id() regex on key is now redundant — use rec['organoid_id'] directly

Acceptance: analysis/paper_2026_04/descriptive_stats.py runs end-to-end and reproduces Table 1 (all 5 metabolite means match paper). Sanity-check Table 3 LightGBM Dy30 = 0.9444.
close_reason: Resolved earlier in branch (commits 8cdaff4, af20cd1, e12dbf9) — data_loader reads normalized schema correctly. Verified via descriptive_stats end-to-end run this session: Glucose mean 11.622, all 5 metabolite means within 0.01 of paper values.

title: Thread loss_fn + scheduler_factory through train.run_phases

The shared train.run_phases (analysis/imagequality_classification/train.py) already accepts loss_fn=None via epoch_loop. Extend it to also accept an optional scheduler_factory(opt) callable; default behavior unchanged. Then delete the dinov2-local run_phases.
acceptance_criteria: train_model_dinov2.py imports run_phases from .train; local run_phases removed; both train_model_accuracy and train_model_dinov2 produce identical metrics on the same input.

title: Stratify prediction performance by vote strength (5-0 / 4-1 / 3-2)

Re-run the good/bad per-day analyses across these strata and report balanced accuracy + the vote-split distribution (with examples). Cohorts already encode consensus thresholds (strong-consensus >=4/5, full = majority); add a 3-2 stratum. Matches STATUS 'performance stratified by vote confidence' + 'vote split distribution'. Feeds paper epic (2025-promega-mini-test-eyq). Owner: Nick. Source: PromegaPlan.docx ('Big one: incorporate strength as a certainty measure').

title: Merge-level conflict logic clears labels across days, not just splits
description:
**Where:** `pipeline/merge/normalized_records.py:184-202` (`_get_organoid_labels` + `_register_split_conflict`).

**Symptom:** 13 Dy30 records currently have `label = {}` instead of a populated label. 12 are intentional (6 split-organoid pairs × 2 splits — the conflict logic correctly clears both halves when split_1 and split_2 disagree). The 13th is **`BA1 96_1 Dy30 E10`** — a non-split organoid with conflicting survey verdicts at Dy28 vs Dy30:

- Dy28 survey: 4 Not Acceptable / 1 Acceptable → bucket says "Not Acceptable"
- Dy30 survey: 4 Acceptable / 1 Not Acceptable → bucket says "Acceptable"

The merge logic groups records by `organoid_id` (BA + well, no day component), so when both Dy28 and Dy30 carry definitive but disagreeing labels, all 11 days' labels for E10 are cleared.

**Why this matters:**

1. **Function name is misleading** — `_register_split_conflict` and the warning message ("Split label conflict") imply this is about split organoids. It actually fires on any cross-day disagreement.

2. **IDOR considers E10 classified** — `BA1_96_1_Dy30_E10_nosplit_nostitch` is in column 2 of `2026_04_Organoid_list_idor.csv` ("organoids that reached Day 30 and were classified"). Per the partner's data conventions, Dy30 is the canonical classification day. Cross-day disagreements at earlier days shouldn't invalidate the Dy30 verdict.

3. **`compute_survey_majority` is being asked to do something it can't reason about**. Each call only sees one day's evaluations. The cross-day conflict is detected at `_get_organoid_labels` time, but by then the per-day labels are already computed. Treating two non-Dy30 / Dy30 disagreements as equivalent to two split-organoid disagreements conflates two different sources of disagreement.

**Proposed fix:** scope the conflict-detection logic to:
  - Splits at the same day (split_1 vs split_2 at Dy30 disagreeing — clear)
  - Same physical record across pre-normalization views

Don't trigger on cross-day disagreement; only Dy30 (or whatever `LABEL_DAY` is) should be the authoritative source for the organoid-level label. If Dy28 and Dy30 disagree, prefer Dy30 (the canonical classification day).

**Acceptance:**
- After the fix, `BA1 96_1 Dy30 E10` has `label.value = "Acceptable"` (the Dy30 verdict).
- The 12 split-pair clears (BA2 96_1 D10 split_1/_2, BA2 96_2 B3 split_1/_2, etc.) remain cleared — they're inter-split conflicts, not cross-day.
- The function and warning message are renamed to reflect the actual scope (e.g. `_register_split_label_conflict` → keep, but only fire on splits).

**Discovered via:** `make analysis-verify-idor` then probing `label = {}` records by hand. See commit 0bbc528 for the surrounding work.
